evals.prompt: ok i'm starting a new session on my forgepay project. claude.md is at the root, i finished sprint 2 last week (stripe connect onboarding). can you run sprint 3 for me? it's the double-entry ledger
evals.expected_output: Boots: reads CLAUDE.md + SPRINT_STATUS.md, runs pre-flight scan on existing codebase, announces Sprint 3 plan with codebase context + TaskCreate per deliverable, implements data layer first using patterns from existing code, runs quality gates + full regression suite, stages specific files, commits, outputs metrics + retro, updates SPRINT_STATUS.md
expectations: Reads CLAUDE.md before writing any code
Reads SPRINT_STATUS.md to confirm Sprint 2 is done
Runs pre-flight codebase scan — finds existing services/models to match patterns
Announces plan with 'Codebase context' section showing discovered patterns
Creates tasks with TaskCreate for each deliverable
Marks tasks in_progress/completed via TaskUpdate as work progresses
Implements data layer (schema/migrations) before business logic
Mirrors existing code patterns (naming, exports, error handling)
Uses build/lint/test commands from CLAUDE.md, not hardcoded ones
Runs full test suite (not just Sprint 3 tests) for regression check
Stages specific files with git add, never git add -A
Commits with conventional message referencing Sprint 3
Reports post-sprint metrics (files changed, lines, test count)
Writes retro in SPRINT_STATUS.md (smooth, friction, watch)
Updates SPRINT_STATUS.md marking Sprint 3 complete with metrics

evals.expected_output: Reads SPRINT_STATUS.md, identifies last completed sprint, executes the next one following full protocol with all advanced protocols
expectations: Reads SPRINT_STATUS.md without being told to
Correctly identifies which sprint is next
Does not ask the user which sprint to run
Runs pre-flight codebase scan before implementing
Follows the full execution protocol with all 6 phases
Creates TaskCreate items for the sprint deliverables
Runs cross-sprint regression check
Outputs metrics and retro at close

evals.prompt: resume — i had to close claude last night mid-sprint 4, i was in the middle of the solana transaction builder
evals.expected_output: Reads SPRINT_STATUS.md Handoff section including saved codebase context, resumes from exact step where session ended, does not restart sprint or re-scan codebase
expectations: Reads SPRINT_STATUS.md Handoff section
Uses saved 'Codebase context' from handoff instead of re-scanning
Resumes from the exact last action, not from Sprint 4 start
Does not re-implement already completed sprint 4 tasks
Recreates TaskCreate items for remaining work only
Continues the commit trail correctly

evals.prompt: the context window is getting full, can you save our progress and wrap up cleanly
evals.expected_output: Commits partial work with wip commit (staging specific files), writes detailed Handoff in SPRINT_STATUS.md including codebase context and remaining tasks, tells user how to resume
expectations: Commits partial work with specific file staging (not git add -A)
Uses wip() commit message format
Writes Handoff section in SPRINT_STATUS.md
Handoff includes: last action, next action, files modified, remaining tasks
Handoff includes codebase context so next session skips re-scanning
Tells user exactly what to say to resume

evals.prompt: i want to start a new project from scratch, i have an architecture doc in notion but no claude.md yet. where do i start?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT try to run a sprint. Offers to help structure a CLAUDE.md with sprint definitions. Explains minimum structure needed.
expectations: Does not attempt to run a sprint without CLAUDE.md
Offers to help create CLAUDE.md
Explains minimum CLAUDE.md structure needed for sprint-runner
Does not fabricate sprint definitions

evals.expected_output: Reads SPRINT_STATUS.md, identifies all remaining sprints, executes them sequentially with full protocol per sprint, commit between each, pause between sprints
expectations: Reads SPRINT_STATUS.md to identify completed and remaining sprints
Executes sprints in order, not in parallel
Runs pre-flight scan once, reuses codebase context across sprints
Commits after each sprint individually
Runs cross-sprint regression after each sprint
Updates SPRINT_STATUS.md with metrics + retro after each sprint
Pauses between sprints to ask user for confirmation before next
Does not skip any phase of the execution protocol for any sprint

evals.prompt: run sprint 3 — the typescript build is failing with 15 type errors after i implement the services
evals.expected_output: Does not brute-force or use 'any' to silence errors. Reads error output, fixes root causes, re-runs typecheck. If stuck after 3 attempts, stops and asks user.
expectations: Reads the full typecheck error output
Fixes type errors at root cause, not with 'any' or 'as' casts
Re-runs typecheck after fixes to verify
Does not proceed to commit while errors exist
If stuck after multiple attempts, stops and asks user for guidance
Does not disable strict mode or loosen tsconfig

evals.expected_output: Treats 'continue' same as 'next sprint' — reads SPRINT_STATUS.md, finds next sprint, runs it with full protocol
expectations: Does not ask 'continue what?' — reads SPRINT_STATUS.md automatically
Identifies the next sprint correctly
Follows full boot sequence and execution protocol
Creates TaskCreate items for tracking

evals.prompt: sprint 2 has a task to add a .env var for STRIPE_SECRET_KEY. make sure it's handled properly
evals.expected_output: Adds the env var to .env.example only (never to .env), documents it, ensures it's read via process.env at runtime, never hardcoded. Auto .env.example sync catches any missing vars.
expectations: Never writes actual secret values to any committed file
Adds placeholder to .env.example with descriptive comment
Reads the value from process.env in code
Does not stage .env files
Secret scan in commit protocol would catch any mistake
Auto .env.example sync verifies all process.env refs have entries

evals.prompt: run sprint 5 — it's a full-stack feature with auth, API routes, services, database schema, and a React frontend. about 20 files total
evals.expected_output: Detects sprint is too large for single context. Proposes splitting into sub-sprints by layer (data, services, API, frontend). Asks user for confirmation before starting.
expectations: Estimates context budget before starting implementation
Identifies sprint as 'too large' (15+ files)
Proposes sub-sprint split by layer
Does NOT start implementing before user confirms the split
Each sub-sprint would commit independently
Sub-sprints follow full execution protocol

evals.prompt: run sprint 3 — i already have src/services/auth.ts and src/services/payment.ts in the codebase. this sprint adds src/services/ledger.ts
evals.expected_output: Pre-flight scan reads existing services, extracts their patterns (error handling, exports, naming), and mirrors them exactly in the new ledger service
expectations: Scans existing src/services/ before writing ledger.ts
Reads at least one existing service file to extract patterns
Reports codebase context in ANNOUNCE phase
New ledger.ts mirrors existing service structure
Reuses existing error types, utilities, and patterns
Does not introduce new patterns that contradict existing ones

evals.prompt: sprint 4 is done but i think it broke the auth tests from sprint 2. can you check?
evals.expected_output: Runs full test suite, identifies failing sprint 2 tests, fixes the regression in sprint 4 code (not by modifying sprint 2 tests), re-runs until all pass, commits the fix
expectations: Runs full test suite to identify regression
Does NOT modify sprint 2 tests to make them pass
Fixes the issue in sprint 4 code that broke the contract
Re-runs full suite to confirm everything passes
Commits the regression fix as a separate commit
Updates SPRINT_STATUS.md with the fix

evals.expected_output: Recognizes 'let's build' as a sprint trigger. Reads CLAUDE.md + SPRINT_STATUS.md, determines next sprint, runs full protocol.
expectations: Recognizes 'let's build' as a trigger for sprint execution
Does not ask 'build what?' — reads SPRINT_STATUS.md automatically
Follows full boot sequence
Runs pre-flight scan and all advanced protocols
